title: prunus mexicana var. polyandra
intro: Prunus mexicana var. polyandra, also known as Mexican plum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Mexican plum include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, digestive problems, and skin issues. Herbal preparations of Mexican plum include tea infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to support immune function, promote wound healing, and soothe digestive discomfort.
